input_row.title: Ensure Private Endpoints are used to access {service}
input_row.description: Use private endpoints to allow clients and services to securely access data located over a network via an encrypted Private Link. To do this, the private endpoint uses an IP address from the VNet for each service. Network traffic between disparate services securely traverses encrypted over the VNet. This VNet can also link addressing space, extending your network and accessing resources on it. Similarly, it can be a tunnel through public networks to connect remote infrastructures together. This creates further security through segmenting network traffic and preventing outside sources from accessing it.
input_row.rationale: Securing traffic between services through encryption protects the data from easy interception and reading. Impact: A Private Endpoint costs approximately US$7.30 per month. If an Azure Virtual Network is not implemented correctly, this may result in the loss of critical network traffic.
input_row.remediation: Default Value: By default, Private Endpoints are not created for services. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/create-private-endpoint-portal 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/create-private-endpoint- cli?tabs=dynamic-ip 4. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/create-private-endpoint- powershell?tabs=dynamic-ip 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-2-secure-cloud-native-services-with-network-controls Additional Information: A NAT gateway is the recommended solution for outbound internet access.
input_row.impact: A Private Endpoint costs approximately US$7.30 per month. If an Azure Virtual Network is not implemented correctly, this may result in the loss of critical network traffic.
input_row.reference: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-network/virtual-networks-overview 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/create-private-endpoint-portal 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/create-private-endpoint- cli?tabs=dynamic-ip 4. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/private-link/create-private-endpoint- powershell?tabs=dynamic-ip 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-network- security#ns-2-secure-cloud-native-services-with-network-controls Additional Information: A NAT gateway is the recommended solution for outbound internet access.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_storage_service_private_endpoint_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program will check for the use of private endpoints to access Azure Storage Services. It will perform the following checks:

- Utilize Azure Resource Manager (ARM) APIs or Azure CLI commands to enumerate all storage accounts in the specified subscriptions or resource groups. For example, the CLI command to list storage accounts is:
  ```
  az storage account list --query "[].{Name:name, ResourceGroup:resourceGroup}" -o table
  ```

- For each storage account retrieved, the program will then check the configuration for private endpoints using the following ARM API endpoint:
  ```
  GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/{resource-group}/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/{storage-account-name}/privateEndpoints?api-version=2021-04-01
  ```

- The program will validate the property `privateEndpointConnections` for each storage account. The expected configuration property to check should indicate that a private endpoint connection exists:
  - `privateEndpointConnections[*].privateLinkServiceConnectionState.status` should be set to `Approved`.

- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The storage account has one or more approved private endpoint connections.
  - **Fail:** The storage account does not have any approved private endpoint connections.

- The expected API responses must confirm the existence of private endpoints as described in the Azure documentation, ensuring secure access and compliance with the guideline.

By following this automated approach, organizations can systematically verify compliance with the control to ensure private endpoints are utilized properly for Azure Storage Services.
